articles.title: Double crisis: Odisha battles severe heatwave and spreading diarrhea outbreak - ODISHA RAY
articles.full_text:
Double crisis: Odisha battles severe heatwave and spreading diarrhea outbreak
Bhubaneswar (24/05/2026) By Lopamudra Pradhan: Odisha is currently facing a dual health crisis as extreme heatwave conditions are being compounded by a rising outbreak of diarrhea.Reports from multiple regions indicate that the combination of blistering temperatures and waterborne illness has caused significant distress, with at least four deaths confirmed so far. The situation is particularly severe in areas like Jajpur and Sundargarh.Following a community feast, many residents fell ill with severe diarrhea, leading to dozens of hospitalizations.Health officials have responded by deploying medical teams to affected villages to collect food and water samples, distribute ORS, saline, and preventive medication, and raise awareness about hygiene and sanitation among the villagers. Medical teams are working actively to treat dehydrated patients, providing necessary fluids and prophylactic doses of doxycycline to those at risk.Despite these efforts, residents remain worried about the spread of the disease and the adequacy of available facilities as the number of patients continues to rise in the wake of the severe summer heat. Beyond the diarrhea outbreak, the state is under immense pressure from a prolonged heatwave that has gripped the region.Experts and officials note that dehydration and heat-related complications are major concerns, with neighboring states like Andhra Pradesh and Telangana also reporting high numbers of casualties due to the intense summer temperatures.The public is being urged to stay cautious and follow health safety guidelines.
